summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for launching folder windows in a separate process. Microsoft Open Specifications documents SeparateProcess under Explorer\Advanced with 1 = enable and 0 = disable, the 25H2 default hive exports the same value as 0, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\SeparateProcess with both Data:0 and Data:1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Launch Folder Windows in a Separate Process
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can run folder windows in the same process or split them into separate Explorer processes.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change this when they want to isolate folder windows for troubleshooting or stability, or when they prefer the default lighter single-process behavior.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it can isolate crashes between windows but may use more memory; disabling it keeps Explorer on the default lighter path.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications maps the separateProcess folder option to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\SeparateProcess and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled. The 25H2 default hive exports SeparateProcess = 0 in the Explorer\Advanced block, and the raw 25H2 dump lists the same value under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing SeparateProcess = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing SeparateProcess = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1.

allowed_values.label: Use the default shared Explorer process model
allowed_values.meaning: Folder windows are not forced into separate Explorer processes. Microsoft documents 0 as the disabled state, the 25H2 default hive exports SeparateProcess = 0, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Launch folder windows in a separate process
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer uses the separate-process mode for folder windows. Microsoft documents 1 as the enabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the documented 0/1 semantics against the live Explorer runtime surface on Win25H2Clean.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes SeparateProcess through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer\Advanced value that the runtime probe validated.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default Explorer preference
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user Explorer Advanced settings on the 25H2 default hive snapshot

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg exports SeparateProcess = 0 in the default Explorer\Advanced block.

windows_defaults.notes: This is current 25H2 default-hive corroboration rather than a guarantee for every historical build.

recommended_profiles.label: Default shared Explorer process model
intended_for: Systems that prefer the default lighter Explorer behavior
avoid_for: Troubleshooting sessions that want folder-window process isolation

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state and the 25H2 default-hive export.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the default lighter Explorer process model.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You intentionally want folder windows isolated in separate processes.
tradeoffs: Keeps Explorer on the default shared-process path.

recommended_profiles.label: Separate Explorer processes
intended_for: Users who want folder-window process isolation
avoid_for: Users who prefer the default lighter Explorer process model

states.rationale: This matches the documented enabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want folder windows launched in a separate Explorer process.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want the extra Explorer process overhead.
tradeoffs: Can isolate folder-window failures more cleanly.
May use more memory than the default shared-process model.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptionsVista separateProcess
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps separateProcess to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\SeparateProcess and states that it MUST be 1 to enable or 0 to disable. The description says the option launches folder dialogs in separate processes.

evidence.title: 25H2 default hive corroboration for SeparateProcess
evidence.summary: The 25H2 default hive exports SeparateProcess = 0 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced, and the raw 25H2 dump lists the same value name under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer SeparateProcess runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote SeparateProcess = 0 and SeparateProcess = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\SeparateProcess with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the 0/1 semantics, the 25H2 default hive corroborates the current default state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states on this build. This is a low-risk user-scope Explorer preference, but it is more of an advanced stability/troubleshooting toggle than a general-user tweak.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\SeparateProcess ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\SeparateProcess ... Data: 1; RESTORED_VALUE=0
validation_proof.notes: The result file was copied back to the host workspace during validation.
